_comment: Audited inventory of native NotificationRouter *producer* call sites -- the code paths that
emit a bottom-right notification instead of a transient modal message box (docs/design/
07-feedback.md 7.5). It is a source-implementation ledger only: it does not claim a native
build, notification pixels, or runtime interaction. Each producer is verified fail-closed
against real (comment-stripped) source by bin/check-notification-producer-contract.py and its
mutation suite bin/test_notification_producer_contract.py.
Scope: this contract validates ONLY the registered producers and the shared router seam. It
deliberately does NOT try to prove that every legacy dialog has been routed -- that unrouted
backlog is tracked by dialog-notification-policy.csv. It fails on an unreal producer (missing
file, function, router call, or source literal) or a mislabeled one (wrong severity, a source
outside the SafeDisplayText allowlist, or informational_only cleared).
A producer may also declare an optional 'wiring_markers' list of {file, pattern, note} entries.
The field is optional -- producers without it (the Batch-A viewprn/newhelp notices) stay valid --
but when present the checker requires every anchored pattern to exist as real (comment-stripped)
source. That binds REACHABILITY, not just existence: a partial revert that removes only a
producer's arming/consumption/guard site leaves the producer function defined but as dead code,
which the existence checks alone would still pass; the wiring markers fail closed on it instead.
Every producer routes purely informational text (a status/acknowledgement), so it is never a
modal input/destructive/credential/security prompt: informational_only is invariably true and
the router's Classify keeps the informational case non-modal. Display text is fixed, translated
and carries no document data, so it uses NotificationPrivacy::SafeDisplayText under an approved
source; the store redacts anything else regardless of this hint.

producers.purpose: Printer-busy notice: the one-way abort is reported to the bottom-right stack instead of a modal OK box.
Help-viewer 'no matches' notice: routed to the stack while the help search dialog stays interactive.

First transient action-confirmation: mirrors the Find & Replace 'Replace All' outcome (replacement count / no-match) into the bottom-right stack; Success for a completed replacement, Information for no match.

wiring_markers.note: Arms the one-shot Replace All confirmation flag in CommandHdl_Impl before the synchronous FID_SEARCH_NOW dispatch. A partial revert removing only this arming leaves the producer defined but never armed -- dead code the existence checks alone would still pass.

Consumes the armed flag by calling the producer in the SvxSearchDialogWrapper::SetSearchLabel outcome sink (both the SearchLabel and OUString overloads). Removing the call sites leaves the producer function unreachable while its definition survives.
Material theme opt-in guard read by lcl_IsMaterialFeedbackActive; under any other VCL_FILE_WIDGET_THEME value the whole confirmation path is a no-op, so this literal must remain live.
